summary: Typosquat of dx-db-connector with hidden remote-code execution backdoor
details: Package name `db-dx-connector` transposes the legitimate `dx-db-connector` package (the package's own `repository.url` in package.json points to github.com/divbloxjs/dx-db-connector). The package mirrors the legitimate API (DivbloxDatabaseConnector class with matching method names) but adds an undocumented method `queryDBConnect` in index.js that base64-decodes a hardcoded URL (`https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/ZIAIK`, an anonymous mutable JSON paste host), HTTP-GETs the `content` field, and pipes the response into a detached `node` child process with stdio ignored — i.e. fetches and executes attacker-controlled JavaScript on any caller that invokes the method. The method is not referenced in README or tests, has no JSDoc, and is named to blend in with the legitimate `connectDB`/`queryDB` API surface. The combination of typosquat naming, API mimicry, obfuscated (base64) anonymous-paste URL, and detached/unref'd remote-code execution is a deliberate supply-chain backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
